violations.title: Heading Levels Skip from H2 to H4
violations.description: Blog article headings skip levels, breaking screen reader navigation.
violations.explanation: Headings must follow a logical, sequential order (h1 → h2 → h3). Skipping heading levels creates navigation confusion for screen reader users who rely on heading structure to scan content.

violations.title: Article Meta Text Low Contrast
violations.description: Date, author, and category text uses light gray that fails WCAG contrast.
violations.explanation: Text must have a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1. Metadata text like dates and author names are often styled with very light colors that fail contrast requirements, making them unreadable for users with low vision.

violations.title: Read More Links Lack Context
violations.description: Multiple 'Read more' links without indicating which article they reference.
violations.explanation: Links with generic text like 'Read more' fail accessibility when there are multiple such links on a page. Each link must indicate its destination, either through visible text or aria-label.

fixSnippets.fixed: Read more about Breaking News Article Title

violations.title: Content Not in Landmark Region
violations.description: Some content blocks are not contained within semantic landmark regions.
violations.explanation: All page content should be contained within landmark regions (<header>, <nav>, <main>, <footer>, <aside>) so screen reader users can navigate by landmark.
